title: Quantinuum Expands Global Footprint to Singapore with the Establishment of a New R&D Centre
why:
- Quantinuum announced the establishment of a new R&D and Operations Centre in Singapore, marking its formal expansion into the country.
- The company plans to deploy its Helios quantum computer in Singapore in 2026, providing local researchers and companies access to advanced quantum computing hardware.
- The new Centre aims to accelerate industrial collaboration across sectors such as pharma, materials science, and finance, and strengthen Singapore's local quantum ecosystem and workforce.
- Quantinuum is working with local startups, including Entropica and Squareroot8, to co-develop quantum innovations and applications through partnerships and its Startup Partner Program.
- The initiative supports Singapore’s National Quantum Strategy by advancing long-term R&D capabilities and workforce development, helping to position Singapore as a global hub for quantum technology.
